mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150,000 new jobs per month signals strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150,000, or the signal's IC drops below 0.15 in 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction permits (HOUST) above 1.4 million units signal increased consumer confidence and durable goods demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60d returns in 50%+ of high HOUST periods, or the signal's IC falls below 0.10 in 18-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal central bank effectiveness in controlling price pressures, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL < 300 and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or interaction effect size drops below 0.2 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) below 102.5 signal weakening manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 60%+ of INDPRO < 102.5 periods, or the signal's IC falls below -0.15 in 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling GDP (GDPC1) and rising consumer spending (RSAFS) signals a liquidity trap scenario where fiscal stimulus outpaces economic output, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in 50%+ of GDPC1 < 20200/RSAFS > 680000 periods, or interaction effect size drops below -0.2 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.0% combined with declining payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in 50%+ of UNRATE > 4.0/PAYEMS < 155000 periods, or interaction effect size drops below -0.15 in rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 303.0 combined with stagnant federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signals central bank inaction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 50%+ of CPIAUCSL > 303.0/FEDFUNDS < 5.1% periods, or interaction effect size drops below -0.2 in rolling 3-year windows.
